title: Dendrophthoe copelandii
taxonomy: Dendrophthoe copelandii is a species of parasitic flowering plant in the family Loranthaceae. It is a type of mistletoe native to the Philippines. The classification of Dendrophthoe copelandii is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, the clade Eudicots, and the order Santalales. Dendrophthoe copelandii is a member of the genus Dendrophthoe, which is a group of parasitic plants that derive their nutrients from their host. The exact taxonomic classification may vary depending on the source or recent updates in botanical research.

intro: Dendrophthoe copelandii, also known as the "mistletoe vine," is a parasitic plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of D. copelandii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, D. copelandii is commonly prepared as a decoction, tincture, or tea, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
